behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is summary-write logic interacting with persisted per-dimension findings for the same round (module handoff/state consistency), not a single pure function. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and no model-output quality is involved. Rejected higher levels: behavior text only requires checking stored verdict state before write; it does not require real infra or LLM decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire summary persistence + findings repository in-process (e.g., test DB). Seed one FAIL finding for round R, attempt to store PASS summary for round R, assert write is rejected with validation error and no PASS summary is committed.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears when gate-computation module and storage-backed findings disagree with manual summary text; this is cross-module data-source selection. No real infra needed beyond in-process wiring. No AI output dependency. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about source-of-truth selection in local components, not subprocess/network/model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Set stored findings to include FAIL while injecting/manual summary text says PASS. Run stop-gate computation through real service/repo wiring and assert verdict is FAIL/escalate based on stored findings, with summary text ignored for final verdict.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires policy enforcement plus durable audit-record emission, which is a module boundary (gate/validator + audit store). A unit-only check could miss missing persistence of warning records. No real OS/network requirement; no LLM quality dependency. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not require real external infra or agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke summary-save path without override flag and assert override is rejected. Invoke with override flag and assert operation succeeds but writes an audit warning record containing actor, round, and reason fields.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: Failure is stale-state linkage between fix-commit metadata and review-round metadata; this is cross-module state handoff. Can be reproduced with in-process commit/review metadata fixtures without real git subprocess. No model-output correctness dependency in this behavior text. Rejected System: text requires ordering/association checks, not real OS/git behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: requirement is freshness linkage, not evaluating model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Seed latest fix commit C2 and an older review round tied to C1; assert PASS finalization is blocked. Seed a review round identifier tied to C2 (or later) and assert freshness precondition is satisfied for finalization.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a live review-agent round on updated diff; execution path depends on real agent run presence in the loop. A stubbed constant round can hide failures where orchestration incorrectly treats stale/non-run agent state as fresh. Rejected Workflow: behavior requires one fresh agentic round gate, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence as the assertion target.
behaviors.test_description: Run a live loop with an update commit and no new real review-agent round; assert PASS finalization is blocked. Then run a real review-agent round on that updated diff and assert finalization can proceed (subject to findings).

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is policy engine reading stored findings + round counter/state and producing terminal outcome; this is module wiring/state integration. No real infra required, and no dependency on LLM output content because findings are already stored. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about terminal decision from stored data, not live agent execution.
behaviors.test_description: Seed stored results containing unresolved MUST-FIX findings and set rounds_used == rounds_limit. Execute outcome computation and assert result is escalation, never PASS.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets a live multi-round review workflow: multiple agentic rounds plus final gate decision and escalation emission. Failure can emerge across sequential handoffs (round N findings, exhaustion state, terminal gate). Per MULTI-STEP, this requires Workflow. Rejected lower levels: Integration/Agentic alone do not exercise end-to-end multi-round sequencing across stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a live pipeline across multiple review rounds on evolving diffs until round limit is reached, with MUST-FIX unresolved in stored findings. Assert final state blocks PASS and emits escalation event/record at workflow end.
